sourceMovie.title: Pride
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Pride (2014) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Matthew Warchus's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Billy Elliot
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pride clicked for you, Billy Elliot (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pride?
faq.answer: Try Billy Elliot, Withnail & I, Blinded by the Light—each shares DNA with Pride while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pride?
faq.answer: Pride (2014) is best described as Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pride worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
